title: Business Analyst
description:
Company Description

Experian is a global data and technology company, powering opportunities for people and businesses around the world. We help to redefine lending practices, uncover and prevent fraud, simplify healthcare, create marketing solutions, and gain deeper insights into the automotive market, all using our unique combination of data, analytics and software. We also assist millions of people to realize their financial goals and help them save time and money.

We operate across a range of markets, from financial services to healthcare, automotive, agribusiness, insurance, and many more industry segments.

We invest in people and new advanced technologies to unlock the power of data. As a FTSE 100 Index company listed on the London Stock Exchange (EXPN), we have a team of 22,500 people across 32 countries. Our corporate headquarters are in Dublin, Ireland. Learn more at experianplc.com.

Job Description

Job description

Job Summary


Develop required documentation such as workshop agenda, presentations, gap analysis reports, process flow diagrams, roles/responsibilities.
Identifying areas of process improvement (efficiency and effectiveness) and recommending solutions that detail pros, cons, and risks.
Promoting continuous process improvement practices through process metrics/KPIs, dashboards and role accountabilities.
Managing and communicating process and business requirements to the implementation team as a bridge to ensure that the proposed solutions meet the customer's expectations.
Participate in testing and ensure all the required documentation is in place to support the client and Managed Service team where needed.
Co-ordination and support of client UAT, verifying defects and enhancements and working with the development team to rectify.
Support client process governance activities, enabling the establishment of boards and management frameworks or adapting existing frameworks as appropriate.
Demonstrations of the product for customers
Proven experience in defining and deploying 'to be' best practice processes and in identifying solutions from a people, process, and technology perspective.
Excellent communication skills (both written and verbal) with strong presentation skills
Solid experience in requirements gathering, including experience in creating process documentation.
Demonstrate ability to influence and consult (providing options with pros, cons and risks) while providing thought leadership to stakeholders in solving governance, process or technical problems.
Strong interpersonal skills, customer centric attitude, ability to deal with cultural diversity. Proven team player and team builder.
Ability to effectively communicate with all levels of the organization
Ability to demonstrate analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to mentor novice staff and drive quality, value, and best practices


Qualifications

Qualifications


B. Tech in Computer Science
3 to 5 minimum ServiceNow process consultancy experience with recent focus on the ITOM module and related experience in ServiceNow Enterp Service Management
Hands-on ServiceNow experience meeting client requirements.
Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Management, Information Systems or related discipline preferred.
Drive ServiceNow (ITOM module) process definition, re-engineering, improvement, and gap analysis of current/to-be processes during client workshops with key customer sponsors and stakeholders.


Additional Information

Our uniqueness is that we celebrate yours. Experian's culture and people are important differentiators. We take our people agenda very seriously and focus on what matters; DEI, work/life balance, development, authenticity, collaboration, wellness, reward & recognition, volunteering... the list goes on. Experian's people first approach is award-winning; World's Best Workplaces™ 2024 (Fortune Top 25), Great Place To Work™ in 24 countries, and Glassdoor Best Places to Work 2024 to name a few. Check out Experian Life on social or our Careers Site to understand why.

Experian is proud to be an Equal Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. Innovation is an important part of Experian's DNA and practices, and our diverse workforce drives our success. Everyone can succeed at Experian and bring their whole self to work, irrespective of their gender, ethnicity, religion, colour, sexuality, physical ability or age. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know at the earliest opportunity.

Experian Careers - Creating a better tomorrow together

Find out what its like to work for Experian by clicking here
Show more

title: Remote Business Analyst
description:
About Turing:

Turing is one of the world’s fastest-growing AI companies accelerating the advancement and deployment of powerful AI systems.

Turing helps customers in two ways: Working with the world’s leading AI labs to advance frontier model capabilities in thinking, reasoning, coding, agentic behavior, multimodality, multilinguality, STEM and frontier knowledge; and leveraging that work to build real-world AI systems that solve mission-critical priorities for companies.




Role Overview:

In this role, you will be working on projects to help fine-tune large language models (like ChatGPT) using your strong analytical and English comprehension skills. The ideal candidate should have the ability to read, summarize, and break down large content into smaller logical blocks, conduct research online, validate claims made in content through online research, and work with the LLM (Large Language Models) to solve puzzles! The projects will also help you learn how to leverage AI to be a better analyst. This is your chance to future-proof your career in an AI-first world!




Your role is critical in helping fine-tune and improve large language models (like gpt), and will make you an expert on how to leverage AI to be a better analyst. This is your chance to future-proof your career in an AI-first world!




What does day-to-day look like:

You would spend time answering a variety of interesting analytical questions and creating scenarios that can train the LLM models to get better. Here are a couple of examples that models might get wrong, and You would articulate the correct answer & explanation to enable the models to learn:
Based on a given distribution of sales by month across locations, could you analyze which location has grown the most? (Hint: what time period should we look at? should we account for sudden variability at the beginning?)
In a small town, there are four distinct neighborhoods: Oak, Pine, Maple, and Elm. A postman is assigned to deliver mail and can only deliver to two neighborhoods in one day, with certain rules (e.g. Oak is always visited before Pine). If he delivers to Oak and Elm on the first day, which neighborhoods does the carrier deliver to on the second day?




Note: No other prior specialized domain experience is needed.




Requirements:

English Proficiency: Ability to read and write in English with a high degree of comprehension skills.
Analytical Skills: Good research and analytical skills
Feedback Skills: Ability to provide constructive feedback and detailed annotations.
Creative Thinking: Creative and lateral thinking abilities.
Communication: Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
Independence: Self-motivated and able to work independently in a remote setting.
Technical Setup: Desktop/Laptop set up with a good internet connection.




Preferred Qualifications:

Bachelors degree or undergraduate in Engineering, Literature, Journalism, Communications, Arts, Statistics, or a related field. We are open to candidates who do not have a Bachelor's degree but have experience in the area.
Experience writing professionally (business analysts, research analyst, copywriter, journalist, technical writer, editor, translator, etc.)
Understanding of Excel and Google Suite.
Proficiency in Data interpretation, Logical reasoning and Basic arithmetic is preferred.




Perks of Freelancing With Turing:

Work in a fully remote environment.
Opportunity to work on cutting-edge AI projects with leading LLM companies.
Potential for contract extension based on performance and project needs.




After applying, you will receive an email with a login link. Please use that link to access the portal and complete your profile.




Know amazing talent? Refer them at turing.com/referrals, and earn money from your network.

Show more

title: Business Analyst & Project Manager
description:
This role is for one of the Weekday's clients

Salary range: Rs 500000 - Rs 2000000 (ie INR 5-20 LPA)

Min Experience: 5 years

Location: Hyderabad

JobType: full-time

You will lead the delivery of customer projects by utilizing AI-driven, AI-centered methodologies. Serving as the liaison between clients—mainly based in the UK—and our internal engineering teams, you will ensure top-quality results, clear communication, and comprehensive documentation throughout every phase. This position requires someone capable of fostering genuine trust with Western clients, managing complex project challenges, and motivating teams to achieve ambitious goals. You will blend insightful business analysis with practical project management, all while advocating for our AI-driven delivery approach.

Requirements

What You'll Be Responsible For


Oversee the end-to-end delivery of client service engagements, from initial scoping through to final sign-off
Serve as the main point of contact for Western clients, cultivating trust and long-term partnerships
Create clear, well-organized documentation, including diagrams, prototypes, and detailed specifications
Maintain project momentum by managing timelines, resolving blockers, and ensuring teams meet quality standards
Enable effective communication between clients and internal engineering, design, and AI teams
Keep up to date with trends in software engineering, cloud infrastructure, AI tools, and emerging technologies
Support pre-sales activities by assisting with proposal scoping, effort estimation, and solution presentations
Promote our AI-led delivery philosophy throughout all project processes


What We Are Seeking


More than 5 years of experience in business analysis, project management, or client-facing delivery roles
Demonstrated experience working with UK or Western clients—candidates who have studied or worked in the UK are particularly encouraged to apply
Outstanding verbal and written communication skills, with an ability to quickly establish trust and rapport
Strong skills in documentation and illustration, comfortable with wireframing, prototyping, and technical writing
Familiarity with databases, cloud infrastructure, programming environments, and modern technology stacks
Ability to engage socially, build friendships within teams and with clients, while driving accountability and delivering results
Comfortable managing ambiguity, conducting difficult conversations, and keeping projects on track
A genuine passion for AI, software engineering trends, and how technology is transforming delivery methods
Show more

title: Remote Business Analyst
description:
About Turing:

Turing is one of the world’s fastest-growing AI companies accelerating the advancement and deployment of powerful AI systems.

Turing helps customers in two ways: Working with the world’s leading AI labs to advance frontier model capabilities in thinking, reasoning, coding, agentic behavior, multimodality, multilinguality, STEM and frontier knowledge; and leveraging that work to build real-world AI systems that solve mission-critical priorities for companies.




Role Overview:

In this role, you will be working on projects to help fine-tune large language models (like ChatGPT) using your strong analytical and English comprehension skills. The ideal candidate should have the ability to read, summarize, and break down large content into smaller logical blocks, conduct research online, validate claims made in content through online research, and work with the LLM (Large Language Models) to solve puzzles! The projects will also help you learn how to leverage AI to be a better analyst. This is your chance to future-proof your career in an AI-first world!




Your role is critical in helping fine-tune and improve large language models (like gpt), and will make you an expert on how to leverage AI to be a better analyst. This is your chance to future-proof your career in an AI-first world!




What does day-to-day look like:

You would spend time answering a variety of interesting analytical questions and creating scenarios that can train the LLM models to get better. Here are a couple of examples that models might get wrong, and You would articulate the correct answer & explanation to enable the models to learn:
Based on a given distribution of sales by month across locations, could you analyze which location has grown the most? (Hint: what time period should we look at? should we account for sudden variability at the beginning?)
In a small town, there are four distinct neighborhoods: Oak, Pine, Maple, and Elm. A postman is assigned to deliver mail and can only deliver to two neighborhoods in one day, with certain rules (e.g. Oak is always visited before Pine). If he delivers to Oak and Elm on the first day, which neighborhoods does the carrier deliver to on the second day?




Note: No other prior specialized domain experience is needed.




Requirements:

English Proficiency: Ability to read and write in English with a high degree of comprehension skills.
Analytical Skills: Good research and analytical skills
Feedback Skills: Ability to provide constructive feedback and detailed annotations.
Creative Thinking: Creative and lateral thinking abilities.
Communication: Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
Independence: Self-motivated and able to work independently in a remote setting.
Technical Setup: Desktop/Laptop set up with a good internet connection.




Preferred Qualifications:

Bachelors degree or undergraduate in Engineering, Literature, Journalism, Communications, Arts, Statistics, or a related field. We are open to candidates who do not have a Bachelor's degree but have experience in the area.
Experience writing professionally (business analysts, research analyst, copywriter, journalist, technical writer, editor, translator, etc.)
Understanding of Excel and Google Suite.
Proficiency in Data interpretation, Logical reasoning and Basic arithmetic is preferred.




Perks of Freelancing With Turing:

Work in a fully remote environment.
Opportunity to work on cutting-edge AI projects with leading LLM companies.
Potential for contract extension based on performance and project needs.




After applying, you will receive an email with a login link. Please use that link to access the portal and complete your profile.




Know amazing talent? Refer them at turing.com/referrals, and earn money from your network.

title: Business Analyst II
description:
Summary:

In this role, you'll lead complex Hire-to-Retire and Payroll transformation projects with strategic influence, while providing expert operational support to Centers of Expertise and handling high-impact worker cases—including LOA, Benefits, and worker Relations matters. Drive process excellence, compliance, and continuous improvement across EMEA through AI-enabled innovation and cross-functional partnerships. Become the go-to specialist who applies policies with care, accuracy, and empathy in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.

Responsibilities:
Lead special projects and programs across the Hire-to-Retire lifecycle, including reviewing BRDs, conducting UAT, leading change management, and driving stakeholder communications.
Collaborate with teams to proactively identify and drive operational improvements using AI, automation, RPA, and analytics.
Lead post-project hypercare and stabilization efforts for seamless transitions, issue resolution, and adoption; train and coach ES Operations teams on new processes/tools.
Build and consult with senior operational management and worker Success leaders to shape strategic alignment.
Foster strong cross-functional partnerships with Benefits/LOA, Payroll, worker Relations, and Legal/Compliance stakeholders to ensure project success.
Conduct planned and ad-hoc analyses to support company initiatives; partner on end-to-end SOPs and process maps with cross-functional stakeholders to standardize global operations.
Provide expert guidance to employees and managers on HR policies, prioritizing worker Relations matters.
Lead frontline resolution of workplace concerns and worker Relations cases, recommending solutions and conducting initial investigations.
Support regional HR programs and business partners with policy guidance across inquiries, maintaining accurate records, metrics, and seamless COE collaboration.
Develop, implement, and continuously improve internal team procedures, conducting regular audits to ensure full compliance with Company policies, procedures, and current legislation.
Drive continuous improvement by creating team procedures, identifying efficiencies, and exercising strategic judgment to enhance worker experience and regional compliance.
Requirements:
Project management experience required.
Bachelor's degree (or equivalent experience) required.
4 years consulting on HR policies/processes with employees/managers; prior HR Generalist experience ideal.
HR operations/shared services/centralized HR function experience preferred; high-volume, fast-paced tech environment.
Proven track record with AI, RPA, chatbots, or HR analytics highly desirable.
Required Skills:
Advanced Excel/Google Sheets proficiency.
Case management systems/Workday experience preferred.
Advanced Google Suite expertise.
English fluency (verbal/written) required; additional European languages highly desirable.
Preferred Skills:
Navigates complex ambiguity independently, managing multiple high-priority initiatives with minimal guidance in fast-evolving environments.
Exceptional analytical, quantitative, time management, and communication skills; influences across all organizational levels.
Resilient and highly adaptable; pivots seamlessly while maintaining strategic focus and driving results through influence.
Crafts compelling executive materials and presentations; excels as a collaborative partner who champions tech-enabled HR transformation.

title: Business Analyst
description:
About the Role:

This individual will provide backend office support, data analysis & visualization and reporting assistance to the Business Head. The position involves data collection, report preparation, coordination with sales and field teams, and support in in driving business through analytics in different domains viz sales, competition, mapping, cost benefit analysis, productivity - derived from system.

Collect data from sales teams, branches, and internal departments for ongoing projects and reports.

Proficient in ppt preparation, analytical and reporting skills.
Good coordination and follow-up abilities.
Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet timelines.
Experience in using Analytics for data-based decision making
Opportunity to work with a well-established brand
Strong communication and interpersonal skills




Qualifications and Experience

B. Tech / Management graduate; Master’s degree preferred.
Experience:
5–7 years of experience in reporting, coordination, backend support, or similar roles.

title: Business Analyst - AI
description:
Your potential, unleashed.




India’s impact on the global economy has increased at an exponential rate and Deloitte presents an opportunity to unleash and realize your potential amongst cutting edge leaders, and organizations shaping the future of the region, and indeed, the world beyond.




At Deloitte, your whole self to work, every day. Combine that with our drive to propel with purpose and you have the perfect playground to collaborate, innovate, grow, and make an impact that matters.




The team




You’ll partner with Enabling Area / AI Team to unlock value from data and AI through modern analytics and GenAI capabilities (e.g., RAG, LLM integrations) and strong delivery practices.




Key Responsibilities

• Lead stakeholder interviews, workshops, and process walkthroughs to elicit and document functional/non-functional requirements (BRDs, user stories, acceptance criteria).

• Perform gap analysis, feasibility assessments, and traceability from business goals to product features.

• Shape product vision and roadmap for AI features; prioritize backlogs based on value, feasibility, and risk.

• Run Agile ceremonies (refinement, sprint planning, reviews) and maintain clear release plans and OKRs.

• Translate use cases into LLM/GenAI solutions (e.g., retrieval-augmented generation, prompt engineering, evaluation criteria) with DS/ML engineers.

• Coordinate data availability, model monitoring, and governance (security, privacy, cost/performance) with platform teams.

• Demo increments, validate requirements, and manage UAT with business users; drive adoption and training assets (playbooks, guides).

• Communicate status, risks, and mitigation plans to leadership; support post-launch analysis and continuous improvement.

• Maintain clear artefacts (journey maps, process flows, personas, wireframes) and QA/test plans in partnership with QA leads.




Qualifications

• Education: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Technology, or Business; MBA is a plus.

• Experience: Typically, 3–5 years in Product/BA roles.

• Core: Proven requirements management, backlog ownership, and Agile delivery with enterprise stakeholders.




Skills (Must-have)

• Strong BA toolkit (elicitation, analysis, documentation, UAT).

• Product tools (Jira/ADO/Confluence), and proficiency in MS Office for analysis and communication.

• AI literacy: familiarity with LLMs/GenAI concepts, RAG patterns, evaluation/guardrails, and data governance.

• Collaboration across Data Science, Engineering, UX; excellent stakeholder communication.




Preferred Exposure

• Hands-on with prompt design, GenAI evaluation, vector databases, or model lifecycle (training, deployment, monitoring).

• Experience in cloud platforms (AWS/Azure/GCP) and modern data/ML tooling.




Success Metrics (KPIs)

• Requirement lead time (from intake to signed-off user stories), backlog health, and sprint goal attainment.

• Feature adoption (usage, satisfaction), model performance & cost (for AI features), and defect escape rate/UAT pass-rate.




Working Model

Hybrid, with flexibility for stakeholder workshops, demos, and UAT at office/client sites as needed.




How you’ll grow




Connect for impact




Our exceptional team of professionals across the globe are solving some of the world’s most complex business problems, as well as directly supporting our communities, the planet, and each other. Know more in our Global Impact Report and our India Impact Report.

Empower to lead




You can be a leader irrespective of your career level. Our colleagues are characterised by their ability to inspire, support, and provide opportunities for people to deliver their best and grow both as professionals and human beings. Know more about Deloitte and our One Young World partnership.

Inclusion for all




At Deloitte, people are valued and respected for who they are and are trusted to add value to their clients, teams and communities in a way that reflects their own unique capabilities. Know more about everyday steps that you can take to be more inclusive. At Deloitte, we believe in the unique skills, attitude and potential each and every one of us brings to the table to make an impact that matters.




Drive your career




At Deloitte, you are encouraged to take ownership of your career. We recognise there is no one size fits all career path, and global, cross-business mobility and up / re-skilling are all within the range of possibilities to shape a unique and fulfilling career. Know more about Life at Deloitte.




Everyone’s welcome… entrust your happiness to us




Our workspaces and initiatives are geared towards your 360-degree happiness. This includes specific needs you may have in terms of accessibility, flexibility, safety and security, and caregiving. Here’s a glimpse of things that are in store for you.




Interview tips




We want job seekers exploring opportunities at Deloitte to feel prepared, confident and comfortable. To help you with your interview, we suggest that you do your research, know some background about the organisation and the business area you’re applying to. Check out recruiting tips from Deloitte professionals.

title: Research Analyst
description:
Job Purpose

To recommend and advise investors for various investment ideas from medium to long term perspective by doing quantitative and qualitative analysis of listed companies. To contribute to the team activities in generating investment ideas, creating and maintaining large and complex databases, help in preparing various reports like strategy note, morning note, weekly market updates. These activities will help in driving sales and information dissemination to investors and sales force.

Job Context & Major Challenges

Aditya Birla Money Ltd. (ABML) is primarily a Broking Company, deals in Equity, Commodity & Currency. Workforce of ~800 spread across ~40 locations with customer base of in excess of 3 lacs. Commodity operations are managed through its 100% subsidiary Aditya Birla Commodities Broking limited. ABML is one of the leading securities trading companies in the country. ABML operates in a fragmented equity broking business and has a daily turnover of close to 3,000 Crores with a market share of 1%. ABM works in a highly regulated environment with a host of regulators like SEBI, RBI, AMFI, FMC, IRDA etc. regulating its day to day operations. In addition to the regulator ABML is also subject to close monitoring and inspection through various agencies like NSE / BSE / NCDX / MCX / NSDL / CDSL etc. ABML also operates in a highly competitive market mainly in fragmented industry with ABML competition from major National Broking houses like ICICI Securities, HDFC Securities, Angel Broking, MOFSL etc. and a range of local Broking outfits.

Majority of the broking houses have set up in-house research team that scans companies and stocks as well as analyse macro-economic scenario that impacts the stock market. The Research team at the Fundamental side values the securities based on the growth prospects and attractiveness of the underlying business. It involves analyzing the financials, competitive advantages of the business, quality of management and competitive environment it operates in among other factors. The theory of Research revolves around the fact that the market price of an asset may deviate from its fair value in short term due to extraneous factors such as market trends or investor sentiments, but will tend to move towards its intrinsic value over the long term.

The Key Responsibilities Of Sr. /Research Analyst Are


To build large databases by extracting and collating appropriate datasets from data vendors like Bloomberg, Reuters, Cline, etc.
To analyse large and complex databases of macro and micro information and create well-structured reports.
To disseminate research reports/output to investors/clients helping them take investment decisions.
To provide research support and inputs for consumption by the investment team in building portfolios and prepare reports/advisory for the clients.
To timely work on and generate investment ideas so as to capture maximum gains out of it.


The Primary Challenges Faced Are


To find investible ideas based on rigorous analysis of data/information sourced from quality database available in the market within a sector, given different risk return profiles and competitive advantages of various companies within a sector.
In a an environment, which is constantly moving and changing, the key challenge is to stay abreast with latest updates in a sector and the companies within it, so as to take nimble investment decisions.
The challenge is to clearly communicate the investment thesis for a company/sector in a manner which clearly highlights the investment case and arguments so that the investment team/investors can take an informed investment decision.
Another key challenge of the role is to take relative investment calls when absolute opportunities are more than what can be accommodated in the portfolio


Critical Skillsets are the individual must have a strong knack for financial analysis and equity research. The role involves minor level of client interaction and hence good interpersonal skills are desirable.

Key Result Areas

KRA (Accountabilities) (Max 1325 Characters)Supporting Actions (Max 1325 Characters)

KRA1 To be able to build, maintain and analyze large and complex databases of macro and micro information


To build large databases by extracting/collating appropriate datasets from data vendors like Bloomberg, Reuters, Cline, etc. and organize the data into sub-heads (viz. macros / micros / sectoral / valuations).
To understand the growth trends of the industry by analysing parameters affecting the dynamics of the industry.
To analyze data using top-down approach (international asset class level, country level and sector level) and also on a bottom up basis on assigned sectors to come up with investment ideas for the investment team and clients.


KRA2 To be able to conduct quantitative and qualitative analysis of the companies of specific sectors and participate in team research activities


To analyze companies/sectors using tools like ratio analysis, time series and cross sectional analysis, so as to make a judgment regarding investment potential of the various sectors and the companies within various sectors.
To read relevant materials from the industry body and participate in industry conferences to maintain the sharpness in investment analysis.
To meet the managements of a company and understand the key trends in the sector, the company and its future plan.
To project the earnings of the company in medium and long term.


KRA3 To be able to disseminate research based output to investors/clients helping them take investment decisions


To prepare an investment argument based on the findings of the analysis and help the investment team build a portfolio as per the risk/return framework of the investor.
To contribute to various products which require team efforts like strategic investment report, morning report, morning sales note, concalls, weekly report.
To increase the visibility of the research team by contributing articles to various media reports.

MIS and Chairman MIS

title: Planning Analyst
description:
We are Licious and we are a Bengaluru based meat and seafood company founded in 2015 by Abhay Hanjura and Vivek Gupta, our founders, foodies and friends. We pride ourselves on being India’s most successful D2C food-tech brand operating in 20 cities across the country delighting over 32 lac customers with our de-licious fresh meat and seafood!




Think you have what it takes to be the magic ingredient in the recipe that is Licious? Read on..




Have you got an awesome appetite for it?




Supply & Inventory Planning Support:

● Support conversion of demand forecasts into supply, sourcing, and inventory plans across processing, dispatch, and last-mile nodes.

● Track and improve inventory metrics including availability, wastage, and liquidation, and highlight risks proactively to relevant stakeholders.

● Assist in maintaining inventory norms and planning parameters for fresh and perishable products




Cross-Functional Stakeholder Management

● Act as the primary planning point of contact for operations teams across Processing, Dispatch, and Last Mile for day-to-day planning alignment

● Coordinate closely with Demand Planning, Sourcing, Procurement, and City Operations team

to ensure supply plans are aligned and clearly communicated

● Work with Product and Engineering teams to articulate planning requirements, validate data outputs, and support rollout of new tools or reports

● Facilitate regular planning reviews, sourcing discussions, and issue-resolution forums

with cross-functional stakeholders




Network & Execution Alignment

● Support inventory placement and replenishment decisions by coordinating inputs across

hubs, cities, and central teams.

● Monitor execution vs plan and work with stakeholders to resolve deviations related to capacity, supply constraints, or demand volatility

● Ensure planning decisions are documented, communicated, and understood across teams




Automation & Process Enablement

● Support automation and reporting initiatives by gathering business requirements, validating

logic, and assisting with UAT and adoption

● Work with Product and Engineering teams to ensure planning tools reflect operational realities and constraints.




Business & Initiative Support

● Provide planning and coordination support for new initiatives, pilots, and scale-up programs.

● Assist partner teams in evaluating feasibility, capacity impact, and execution risks.




We hope that you have..

● 1–3 years of experience in supply chain, planning, analytics, or operations roles

(fresh /perishable supply chains preferred)

● Strong stakeholder management and communication skills; ability to work across multiple teams and influence without authority

● High attention to detail with ability to track multiple workstreams simultaneously

● Comfort working with data, dashboards, and planning tools (Excel, SQL, BI tools preferred)

● Structured problem-solving mindset and ability to escalate issues with clarity and context.

title: Performance Sr. Analyst, APAC
description:
Netflix is one of the world's leading entertainment services, with over 300 million paid memberships in over 190 countries enjoying TV series, films and games across a wide variety of genres and languages. Members can play, pause and resume watching as much as they want, anytime, anywhere, and can change their plans at any time.

Our members enjoy an increasingly diverse offering of TV series, films, and games across various languages, devices and genres. The Product Merchandising organization delivers intuitive merchandising and local expertise to make it easy and enjoyable for our members to find and choose what to watch now, and next.

To improve how we accomplish this, we consistently investigate and evaluate our work. The Performance Sr. Analyst role will be responsible for driving custom regional analyses to understand title and catalog promotion, product behavior, and merchandising activations. This role will use self-serve tooling and will partner with Global Merchandising performance teams to develop and continue to scale dashboards for performance tracking.

Success in this role requires an advanced knowledge of distribution metrics and concepts, a curious mindset, and a collaborative spirit. You should be comfortable at the intersection of data and creativity, and thrive at translating information (data, facts) into understanding (stories and takeaways).

In This Role, You Will Be Responsible For


Promotional Distribution analyses for titles, catalog, on-service activations and the effectiveness of merchandising strategies in the APAC markets. This role demands a deep understanding of consumer behavior, promotional funnels, and product performance.
Partnering with local Product Merchandising Leads and Market leaders to plan and measure local activations for priority titles and Netflix’s regional catalog.
Working with the Global Performance team to support efforts to standardize distribution performance reporting, helping identify cross-region best practices to enhance scalability and consistency in analytics. This includes collaborating with the Global Performance team to evolve self-serve Merchandising dashboards/tools.
Serving as a subject matter expert and a primary point of contact for promotional data in the APAC markets, supporting local Product Merchandising teams. This role will also support as a connector and liaison across different regions and the core team.


Qualifications [including specific skills, years of experience, etc]:


5-7 years of experience in analytical and insight roles
Technical expertise in experimentation (AB tests), writing and reading queries (SQL) and metrics understanding
Demonstrated experience with storytelling skills, especially with data analysis (visual slides, memos, synthesizing quantitative and qualitative insights)
Strong communication and presentation skills - ability to influence without authority
Must be a self-starter who can work independently
Experience and knowledge with various APAC markets, understanding the nuances and differences between them
Genuine interest and curiosity in Netflix’s business, product, content, and the entertainment industry


Inclusion is a Netflix value and we strive to host a meaningful interview experience for all candidates. If you want an accommodation/adjustment for a disability or any other reason during the hiring process, please send a request to your recruiting partner.

We are an equal-opportunity employer and celebrate diversity, recognizing that diversity builds stronger teams. We approach diversity and inclusion seriously and thoughtfully. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, ancestry, national origin, caste, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, age, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic makeup, marital status, or military service.

title: Associate - Omni Channel
description:
Associate – Omni Channel




Who are we?




Myntra is India’s leading fashion and lifestyle platform, where technology meets creativity. As pioneers in fashion e-commerce, we’ve always believed in disrupting the ordinary.

We thrive on a shared passion for fashion, a drive to innovate to lead, and an environment that empowers each one of us to pave our own way. We’re bold in our thinking, agile in our execution, and collaborative in spirit.

Here, we create MAGIC by inspiring vibrant and joyous self-expression and expanding fashion possibilities for India, while staying true to what we believe in.

We believe in taking bold bets and changing the fashion landscape of India. We are a company that is constantly evolving into newer and better forms and we look for people who are ready to evolve with us.

From our humble beginnings as a customization company in 2007 to being technology and fashion pioneers today, Myntra is going places and we want you to take part in this journey with us.

Working at Myntra is challenging but fun - we are a young and dynamic team, firm believers in meritocracy, believe in equal opportunity, encourage intellectual curiosity and empower our teams with the right tools, space, and opportunities.




Role Overview

As an Associate Omnichannel, you will serve as the primary liaison between top-tier brands and our internal cross-functional teams (Tech, Operations, Bizfin, and Category). You will be responsible for the end-to-end lifecycle of brand management, from initial onboarding and cataloguing to strategic growth and operational excellence.




Core Responsibilities

Account & Relationship Management

Stakeholder Liaison: Act as the central point of contact for brands and internal departments to ensure seamless communication.
Cataloguing & Go-Live: Oversee listing activities and rigorously maintain Turnaround Time (TAT) schedules for new stores and sub-brands.
Performance Tracking: Maintain detailed trackers to monitor progress and consistently achieve weekly targets.




Strategic Growth & Planning

Brand Strategy: Formulate and execute comprehensive brand strategies to drive market presence.
Pricing & Promotions: Analyze market trends to suggest effective discounting strategies to brand partners.
Financial Planning: Propose marketing budgets aligned with GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) and NGMV targets.
Inventory Optimization: Monitor live price points and ensure healthy inventory levels are maintained at all times.




Operations & Business Development

Operational Excellence: Resolve seller payout discrepancies and cataloguing issues; escalate and close tech/order flow hurdles in collaboration with the Tech and Ops teams.
Portfolio Expansion: Scale existing brands through store and category expansion while increasing inventory exposure.
New Brand Acquisition: Identify and connect with potential brand partners, guiding them through the entire onboarding and go-live process.

Show more

title: Analyst
description:
About Lowe’s

Lowe’s is a FORTUNE® 100 home improvement company serving approximately 16 million customer transactions a week in the United States. With total fiscal year 2024 sales of more than $83 billion, Lowe’s operates over 1,700 home improvement stores and employs approximately 300,000 associates. Based in Mooresville, N.C., Lowe’s supports the communities it serves through programs focused on creating safe, affordable housing, improving community spaces, helping to develop the next generation of skilled trade experts and providing disaster relief to communities in need. For more information, visit Lowes.com




Lowe’s India, the Global Capability Center of Lowe’s Companies Inc., is a hub for driving our technology, business, analytics, and shared services strategy. Based in Bengaluru with over 4,500 associates, it powers innovations across omnichannel retail, AI/ML, enterprise architecture, supply chain, and customer experience. From supporting and launching homegrown solutions to fostering innovation through its Catalyze platform, Lowe’s India plays a pivotal role in transforming home improvement retail while upholding strong commitment to social impact and sustainability. For more information, visit Lowes India




About the Team

The Merchandising Analysts team partners with U.S. Merchants to enable data-driven category decisions that drive sales, margin, inventory productivity, and omni-channel execution excellence. The team serves as the analytical engine supporting category strategy, product line reviews, vendor negotiations, inventory optimization, product setup governance, and cross-functional issue resolution across stores and online.

This team is a critical bridge between Merchandising, Inventory Planning, Supply Chain, Stores, Online, Finance, and Vendor partners to ensure accurate execution of product, cost, promotional, and stocking strategies.




Job Summary

The Merchandising Analyst supports the U.S.-based Merchant team and plays a critical role in executing system-driven vendor and item setup processes, ensuring timely and accurate onboarding for both core and online-only assortments. This role requires close collaboration with U.S. Merchants to understand category-specific online promotional calendars, strategies, and business priorities. The Merchandising Analyst partners with vendors to plan and execute item-level promotions aligned with event strategies, ensuring achievement of margin expectations and promotional depth targets.




Roles & Responsibilities

1.Category & Business Insights

Support Merchants in evaluating opportunities to drive incremental revenue and margin improvement.
Develop presentations and analytical insights for merchants and vendors to support strategic planning, vendor negotiations, and execution of merchandising initiatives.
Provide actionable insights on category performance, vendor economics, promotional effectiveness, and assortment productivity.
Deliver weekly reporting to the Merchant team, including sales, margin, and inventory productivity across category hierarchies.




2.Vendor and cross functional stakeholder management

Initiate vendor and item setup activities based on inputs from the U.S. Merchant team
Conduct freight analysis to support 2P/3P channel decisions.
Partner with cross-functional teams to resolve vendor or product-related issues impacting availability or performance, including tracking and closure of tickets.
Support Merchants in vendor negotiations by ensuring cost justification documentation is complete, validated, and submitted to relevant stakeholders.




3.Omni Execution of Products/Items

Collaborate with SSC merchandising teams to understand the online promotional calendar and event requirements.
Engage with vendors to source online promotional items and ensure alignment with event strategies, including margin and promotional depth targets.
Ensure featured deals are accurately set up, vendor-funded where applicable, and meet profitability expectations.
Review and validate proforma documents, vendor agreements, buying terms, and allowances for accuracy and compliance.
Partner with vendors to ensure omni-channel readiness, including product attributes, images, descriptions, and digital content.
Manage and validate cost changes to ensure alignment with margin expectations and profitability guardrails.
Maintain accuracy and integrity of product data, including cost, vendor agreements, and stocking information.




Years of Experience

Minimum 2 years of total work experience in retail / merchandising domain.




Education Qualification & Certifications

Graduate/Bachelor's Degree - in business, marketing, finance, statistics or related field (B.com / BBM / MBA / Retail Operations Management).




Primary Skills

Experience working with merchants, vendors, e-commerce vendor and item lifecycle. management, pricing / promotion management, category management.
Understanding of retail financials (sales, margin, inventory productivity, vendor economics).
Good communication and presentation skills.
Strong project and process management skills.
Proficient in - Excel, PowerPoint and Word, Data analysis.




Secondary Skills

SQL, Power Query, Power BI, Data visualization.




Lowe’s is an equal opportunity employer and administers all personnel practices without regard to race, color, religious creed, sex, gender, age, national origin, mental or physical disability or medical condition, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic information, or any other category protected under state or local law. Lowe’s wishes to maintain appropriate standards and integrity in meeting the requirements of the Information Technology Act’s privacy provisions.

criteria.Industries: Online and Mail Order Retail, Retail, and Retail Apparel and Fashion

title: Commercial Business Analyst
description:
Job Description



WHAT YOU'LL DO



As Commercial Business Analyst, you are responsible for driving the strategic insight work and analysis of our merchandising through advanced data, customer and consumer insights in regard to our product. You will independently be able to set the scope and initiate analysis deepening on the need of the merchandising organization, demonstrate leadership, analytical prowess, and a deep understanding of industry trends.​



​You have a deep interest in retail analytics, inventory optimization and merchandising strategy development. You are customer and sales-driven professional with a strategic mindset. You thrive working in a fast pace, in and agile environment. You are natural team player that strives to foster collaboration across channels, roles and functions.​



Key responsibilities​



Conduct in-depth analysis of sales trends, customer behaviors and market dynamics to identify growth opportunities and areas for improvement; using statistical models and forecasting techniques to drive data driven decision-making.​



You stay abreast of industry trends, competitor product strategies, and emerging technologies to challenge current plans, maintain competitive advantage and support executional excellence by supporting with market intelligence in how to position, activate and optimize our offers.​



Analyze (and align with Customer Activation) SEO insights to support the SEO with our assortment strategy in close collaboration with merchandising managers / ecommerce leads as well as drive insights to support assortment strategy for all our channels; retail, digital and marketplaces.​



You drive projects and build business cases for new product categories, sales channels and other business opportunities to support new sales and profit growth.​



Follow up on our full (omni, retail and ecom) commercial initiatives and build strong case for improvement in our discount strategy, leveraging web analytics, customer insights, sales and cost data; enabling best possible commercial decision.



Please click here for complete role description



​WHO YOU'LL WORK WITH



You will collaborate with the colleagues from Merchandising team.



WHO YOU ARE​



To be successful in the role as Commercial Business Analyst, you should have strong commercial focus and are insight and data driven, with proven ability to drive results and work towards goals. You should have a broad merchandising knowledge and commercial experience that understands the total picture from customer demands on our offers.​



​What you need to succeed:​



Bachelor's degree, preferably in Economics or Business degree/relevant qualifications.​



Strong in driving commercial product excellence, strategic and analytical, with deep experience in business case building as well as follow up and estimate performance.​



Understanding of product, stocks and lifecycle management.​



High level of salesmanship and analytical proficiency; is an advance excel user.​



Effective collaborator with strong stakeholder management and communication skills.



Knowledge & experience in the Indian fashion retail.



WHO WE ARE​​



H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​ ​



We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​ ​



WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE ​​



At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. ​​



You will receive 25% off H&M Group brands; H&M, COS, Monki, Weekdays, Arket, & Other Stories
We are an inclusive company where you are encouraged to be yourself at work


Additional Information



This is a full-time position, reporting to Regional Head of Merchandising.



Apply by sending in your CV in English as soon as possible, but no later than April 24th, 2026. Due to data policies, we only accept applications through career page.



Show more

title: Product Analyst
description:
About the Team:




You’ll work with a team that’s reimagining how India interacts with a bank - powered by some of the smartest minds and the most intuitive tech in the ecosystem.




If you love impact, autonomy, and velocity, this is your playground




About the Role:




As an Analyst at slice, you’ll turn data into decisions. You’ll work with teams across Growth, Credit, Product, and Leadership to uncover insights, solve real business problems, and drive strategy. If you’re excited about using data to fuel impact at scale, this role is for you.




What You’ll Be Do:




Own the Full Analytics Spectrum




Think from First Principles: Break problems down to their core, use data and reasoning to uncover insights that move the business and make life simpler for our customers.

Descriptive – Build clear, compelling visualizations and dashboards.
Diagnostic – Dig into the why behind trends and performance.
Prescriptive – Turn insights into recommendations that drive smart decisions.

Drive Impact at Scale

Take full ownership of complex, high-impact problems that shape key org KPIs and influence strategic direction.
Lead end-to-end analytics projects in close partnership with Product, Business, and Strategy teams — from framing the problem to shaping solutions that drive measurable outcomes.

Partner with Leaders Across Teams

Work side by side with functional leaders and business partners across Growth, Retention, Product, Strategy, Engineering, Marketing, Fraud, and Ops — asking the right questions, challenging assumptions, and helping define the real business problems worth solving.
Tell Stories That Drive Action: Turn complex data into simple, actionable stories — crafting solutions that power big decisions and deliver real impact at scale.




What You'll Need:




Hustle + Heart: You’ve got the drive to build data-driven solutions that touch millions of lives - and the curiosity to keep asking “what’s next?”
1-3 years of experience solving complex business problems, uncovering insights, and driving key decisions that move metrics at scale.
AI-Native Mindset: Comfortable using tools like Cursor, Claude, or CoPilots to boost your speed, scale, and smarts.
You speak data fluently: Python and Advanced SQL are your second languages, and you’re comfortable in PySpark, R, or Excel when the situation calls for it.
Problem-solver at your core: You love breaking big, messy problems into bite-sized pieces — and crafting smart, scalable solutions that stick.
Natural Storyteller: You don’t just present numbers — you craft narratives that align stakeholders and inspire action.
Always learning: Whether it’s a new tool, technology, or technique, you’re excited to expand your toolkit.
Visualization is your superpower: Whether it’s dashboards or decks, you know how to turn numbers into a story people instantly get.




Life at slice:




Life so good, you’d think we’re kidding:




Competitive salaries. Period.
An extensive medical insurance that looks out for our employees & their dependants. We’ll love you and take care of you, our promise.
Flexible working hours. Just don’t call us at 3AM, we like our sleep schedule.
Tailored vacation & leave policies so that you enjoy every important moment in your life.
A reward system that celebrates hard work and milestones throughout the year. Expect a gift coming your way anytime you kill it here.
Learning and upskilling opportunities. Seriously, not kidding.
Good food, games, and a cool office to make you feel like home. An environment so good, you’ll forget the term “colleagues can’t be your friends”.
We believe in equality. Period.




At slice, we are committed to building a diverse and talented workforce. We never discriminate on the basis of race, sex, religion, colour, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, medical condition, disability, or any other class or characteristic protected by the applicable law.




We consider all qualified job-seekers with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the applicable law. Additionally, we are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with physical or mental disabilities in order to participate in the job application or interview process, perform essential job functions, and receive other benefits and privileges of employment.




Come join our crew!




About us




slice

A new bank for a new India




slice’s purpose is to make the world better at using money and time, with a major focus on building the best consumer experience for your money. We’ve all felt how slow, confusing, and complicated banking can be. So, we’re reimagining it. We’re building every product from scratch to be fast, transparent, and feel good, because we believe that the best products transcend demographics, like how great music touches most of us.




Our cornerstone products and services: slice savings account, slice UPI credit card, slice UPI, and slice business are designed to be simple, rewarding, and completely in your control. At slice, you’ll get to build things you’d use yourself and shape the future of banking in India. We tailor our working experience with the belief that the present moment is the only real thing in life. And we have harmony in the present the most when we feel happy and successful together.




We’re backed by some of the world’s leading investors, including Tiger Global, Insight Partners, Advent International, Blume Ventures, and Gunosy Capital.

Show more

title: Data Analyst
description:
Experience with Kafka, GIT, Jenkins, Dynatrace.
Experience with big data technologies such as Hadoop, Spark.
Experience with cloud computing platforms such as AWS, Azure, GCP
Experience with data governance and security
Work with stakeholders to understand their business needs.
Translate business needs into technical requirements.
Communicate requirements to the engineering teams.
Work with the engineering teams to develop and implement solutions that meet the needs of the stakeholders.
Monitor and evaluate the performance of the data platform and make recommendations for improvement.
Stay up to date on the latest data technologies and trends.
Experience working with data engineering, data analytics, and data science teams.
Strong understanding of data modeling, extraction and big data technology
Experience with agile development methodologies
2+ years’ Experience with hands-on data analysis and visualization tools such as Kibana, Grafana, Prometheus, Looker, Lucene Query, big Python, Google Standard SQL.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

title: Senior Associate
description:
Role Overview

The Senior Associate – Strategic Sourcing will be responsible for managing Business Services (BS), Admin procurement requirements covering both OPEX and CAPEX, including office lease, Office fitouts etc. The role will work closely with different teams such as Business Services, Legal, Finance etc. and to ensure timely sourcing, cost optimization, compliance, and seamless execution of procurement activities.

Key Responsibilities

• Manage end‑to‑end procurement activities for BS OPEX and CAPEX requirements. • Handle office leasing activities including coordination with landlords, brokers, legal teams, and internal stakeholders for LOI, lease agreements, registration, renewals, and amendments. • Drive RFP processes, vendor evaluations, commercial negotiations, and contract finalization. • Coordinate with Finance for PO approvals and payment tracking. • Work closely with Legal for contract reviews, compliance, and risk mitigation. • Manage vendor onboarding on Supplier Management tool. • Support project‑related procurement for office fit‑outs, civil works, facilities, and allied services. • Ensure adherence to internal procurement policies, timelines, and governance standards. • Prepare MIS, trackers, dashboards, and regular status updates for leadership.

· Ensure timely submission of Audit Observations.

· Ensure engagement with SBTi-registered vendors and provide guidance to any vendors that are not yet registered with SBTi.

Stakeholder Management

• Business Services Teams • Finance and Accounts • Legal and Compliance • External vendors, landlords, and consultants

Qualifications & Experience

• Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain, Commerce, or a related field. • 5-6 years of relevant experience in procurement / sourcing, preferably in corporate real estate or business services / admin procurement. • Experience handling OPEX and CAPEX procurement categories and office leasing transactions. • Strong understanding of contract management and commercial negotiations.

Key Skills & Competencies

• Strong negotiation and commercial acumen • Knowledge of procurement processes and sourcing best practices

· Knowledge of Oracle P+ and Apttus (for contracting) is an added advantage • Excellent stakeholder and vendor management skills • Good understanding of lease agreements and real estate documentation • Strong analytical, documentation, and MIS skills • Proficient in MS Excel, PowerPoint, and procurement systems • Ability to manage multiple priorities and work under tight timelines.
